_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-40R Prednisone (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-40R: Prednisone — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Anaphylaxis (adjunct to epinephrine — reduces biphasic reaction risk)
Adrenal crisis (when patient can swallow and IV access not yet established)
Respiratory distress — asthma or COPD exacerbation (preferred oral steroid when patient can take PO)
contraindications: Age <6 years (do not give prednisone to children <6 years — use methylprednisolone IV/IM instead)
Inability to take oral medications (vomiting, AMS, swallowing difficulty — use methylprednisolone IV/IM)
precautions: Preferred over methylprednisolone for respiratory distress when patient can take oral medication
Do NOT cut tablets — 50 mg tablets should be swallowed whole
Delayed onset — hours to days for anti-inflammatory effect; not useful for immediate relief
Single prehospital dose is generally safe
expected_effects: Delayed anti-inflammatory effect — reduces airway inflammation
Reduction in biphasic anaphylaxis risk (late-phase reaction)
side_effects: Single dose side effects are generally minimal
notes: Available as 50 mg tablet — swallow whole; do NOT cut
Adult and pediatric >6 years: 50 mg PO
Contraindicated <6 years — use methylprednisolone IV/IM instead
Preferred over IV methylprednisolone for respiratory distress when patient can swallow

dosing.notes: Do not cut tablets; patient must be able to swallow; contraindicated <6 years
